SEARCH
Package io.github.dailystruggle.effectsapi.common.spi

Interface ValueCoercer

All Known Implementing Classes:
BukkitValueCoercer, FabricValueCoercer
public interface ValueCoercer
Per-platform leaf operations of the effects-api-ADR-002 adaptive reading order. Introduced by effects-api-ADR-004 so that effectsapi.common.Effect no longer has to import org.bukkit.*.

Implementations are bound at platform-init time via EffectFactory.setCoercer(ValueCoercer):

  • Bukkit-family: BukkitEffectsInitializer.registerAll() → BukkitValueCoercer
  • Fabric: FabricEffectsInitializer.registerAll() → FabricValueCoercer

Determinism (contractual): canParse(TypeKey, String) must be a pure function of (type, raw) — no I/O, no chunk loads (S-005), no exceptions on rejection.

S-004 (no silent failures): parse(TypeKey, String) must either return a non-null result or throw IllegalArgumentException with a human-readable diagnostic. It must not return null.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    boolean
    canParse(TypeKey type, String raw)
    Cheapest, side-effect-free check: can the raw token be read as the given logical type? Must never throw.
    TypeKey
    classify(Object def)
    Map a defaults-map value to its logical TypeKey.
    Object
    parse(TypeKey type, String raw)
    Parse the raw token into the platform-native object the concrete effect consumes.
    List<TypeKey>
    readingOrder()
    Declared reading order for this platform.
    Object
    resolveReflective(Class<?> targetType, String raw)
    Reflective fallback used by Effect#fixData when classify(Object) returns TypeKey.UNKNOWN — the default value's runtime class is not one of the known logical types and the coercer should try valueOf(String) / getByName(String) / a registry lookup keyed by targetType.

  • Method Details

    • classify

      TypeKey classify(Object def)
      Map a defaults-map value to its logical TypeKey. Implementations should switch on Object.getClass() of def (and its fully-qualified class name when the platform type is registry-backed and not directly importable here).
      Parameters:
      def - the default value, possibly null
      Returns:
      the logical type, never null; returns TypeKey.STRING for null (legacy "permissive" behaviour) and TypeKey.UNKNOWN for anything the coercer doesn't specifically understand

    • canParse

      boolean canParse(TypeKey type, String raw)
      Cheapest, side-effect-free check: can the raw token be read as the given logical type? Must never throw.

    • parse

      Object parse(TypeKey type, String raw)
      Parse the raw token into the platform-native object the concrete effect consumes. Throws IllegalArgumentException on parse failure (never returns null). Never performs chunk I/O (S-005).

    • resolveReflective

      Object resolveReflective(Class<?> targetType, String raw)
      Reflective fallback used by Effect#fixData when classify(Object) returns TypeKey.UNKNOWN — the default value's runtime class is not one of the known logical types and the coercer should try valueOf(String) / getByName(String) / a registry lookup keyed by targetType.
      Returns:
      the resolved object, or null when no resolver matches (caller surfaces an IllegalArgumentException per S-004)

    • readingOrder

      List<TypeKey> readingOrder()
      Declared reading order for this platform. ADR-002's ladder iterates defaults in declaration order; the platform's preferred order is informational and reserved for future cache-promotion tuning.
